entities.summary: Multi-head attention module with GQA, RoPE, and paged KV cache

detail.name: Qwen3Attention
detail.description: Core attention layer. Implements grouped-query attention with rotary embeddings and paged KV cache for efficient inference.

teaches.explain: Split representation into H heads, compute attention independently per head, concatenate. Each head learns different relationship patterns.
Share K/V heads across multiple Q heads (e.g., 8 Q heads per 1 KV head). Reduces KV cache size without significant quality loss.

entities.summary: Configure attention heads, projections, and RoPE parameters

detail.name: __init__
detail.description: Creates Q/K/V projections (fused), output projection, and rotary embedding. Handles GQA head ratios.

teaches.explain: hidden_size / num_heads = head_dim. Each head operates on head_dim dimensions independently. Typical: 128.

entities.summary: Full attention forward: project, rotate, cache, attend, project back

detail.name: forward
detail.description: QKV projection → RoPE → store KV cache → attention → output projection.

teaches.explain: Q,K,V = project(x) → apply RoPE to Q,K → store K,V in cache → attention(Q, cached_K, cached_V) → project output.

entities.summary: SwiGLU MLP block with tensor-parallel gate/up/down projections

detail.name: Qwen3MLP
detail.description: Two-stage MLP: fused gate+up projection → SiLU activation with gating → down projection.

teaches.explain: gate = SiLU(W_gate @ x); output = (gate * W_up @ x) @ W_down. Gated activation improves over plain FFN. Standard in modern LLMs.

entities.summary: Create gate/up (merged) and down projection layers

detail.name: __init__
detail.description: MergedColumnParallel for gate+up, RowParallel for down. Parallelism is transparent to forward pass.

teaches.explain: First linear: column-parallel (no communication). Activation: local. Second linear: row-parallel (all-reduce after). One all-reduce per MLP.

entities.summary: Gate+up projection → SiLU gating → down projection

detail.name: forward
detail.description: Fused gate_up matmul, split and apply SiLU*gate, then down projection with all-reduce.

teaches.explain: x → merged(gate,up) → chunk → SiLU(gate)*up → down_proj. The gating controls information flow through the MLP.

entities.summary: Single transformer block: attention + MLP with residual connections

detail.name: Qwen3DecoderLayer
detail.description: LayerNorm → Attention → residual → LayerNorm → MLP → residual. Repeated N times in the model.

teaches.explain: Apply LayerNorm before (not after) attention/MLP. Stabilizes training. Used by GPT-2+, LLaMA, Qwen, most modern LLMs.
output = layer(x) + x. Gradient flows directly through addition. Enables training of very deep networks (100+ layers).

entities.summary: Compose attention, MLP, and layer norms into one transformer block

detail.name: __init__
detail.description: Creates self_attn, mlp, input_layernorm, post_attention_layernorm as submodules.

teaches.explain: Complex layers built by composing simpler nn.Modules. Each sub-module manages its own parameters. Clean separation of concerns.

entities.summary: Pre-norm attention → residual → pre-norm MLP → residual

detail.name: forward
detail.description: Standard transformer block forward: norm, attend, add residual, norm, MLP, add residual.

teaches.explain: h = x + attn(norm(x)); output = h + mlp(norm(h)). Two residual streams. Each sub-layer adds to the residual.

entities.summary: Stack of transformer decoder layers with embeddings

detail.name: Qwen3Model
detail.description: Composes embedding + N decoder layers + final norm. The backbone without the language model head.

teaches.explain: Model = embedding + layers + norm (reusable backbone). Head = task-specific output layer. Separation enables weight sharing and fine-tuning flexibility.

entities.summary: Instantiate embedding, all decoder layers, and final normalization

detail.name: __init__
detail.description: Creates ModuleList of decoder layers, plus embedding and final RMSNorm.

teaches.explain: Ordered list of modules. Unlike Python list, properly registers sub-modules for parameter tracking, serialization, and device placement.

entities.summary: Embed tokens then pass through all transformer layers sequentially

detail.name: forward
detail.description: tokens → embedding → layer[0] → layer[1] → ... → layer[N] → final_norm → hidden_states.

teaches.explain: Each layer transforms hidden state in place. Output shape identical to input. Enables arbitrary depth without architecture changes.

entities.summary: Complete causal language model: backbone + LM head for next-token prediction

detail.name: Qwen3ForCausalLM
detail.description: Wraps Qwen3Model with parallel LM head. The full model used for text generation.

teaches.explain: Predicts next token given all previous tokens. Unidirectional attention mask ensures each position only sees past. Foundation of GPT-style models.

entities.summary: Combine backbone model with output language model head

detail.name: __init__
detail.description: Creates Qwen3Model + ParallelLMHead. Optionally ties embedding weights.

teaches.explain: CausalLM = backbone(embed + layers + norm) + head(logits). Clean two-level hierarchy.

entities.summary: Full forward pass: embed → decode → logits

detail.name: forward
detail.description: Runs backbone to get hidden states, then computes logits for sampling.

teaches.explain: input_ids → backbone.forward() → hidden_states → compute_logits() → vocabulary scores. End-to-end differentiable.

entities.summary: Project final hidden states to vocabulary logits

detail.name: compute_logits
detail.description: Applies LM head to hidden states. Only computes logits for positions that need sampling (optimization).

teaches.explain: During generation, only the last token position needs logits. Skip computing logits for cached positions. Saves one full vocab-size matmul per cached token.
